title: Eugenia elongata
taxonomy: Eugenia elongata is a species within the genus Eugenia, which belongs to the family Myrtaceae. The genus Eugenia is part of the order Myrtales, and the family Myrtaceae is part of the clade Rosids. Further classification places Myrtaceae in the clade Eurosids I, and Myrtales in the clade Rosids within the larger clade Rosidae. This classification positions Eugenia elongata within the broader context of flowering plant diversity. The comprehensive classification includes kingdom Plantae.
uses: Eugenia elongata has been found to have various medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine. The plant is used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems. Its leaves and fruits are used to make infusions that are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. Additionally, Eugenia elongata is also used to treat digestive problems and skin conditions. More research is needed to fully understand the potential health benefits of the plant.
benefits: Eugenia elongata is a plant species known for its potential health benefits, primarily due to its rich antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
